name: Serverless API
whenToUse: Spiky or unpredictable traffic, pay-per-use economics, and minimal operations: a JSON/REST API with no servers to manage. Best when request volume is bursty and per-request work is short.
burstMechanisms: DynamoDB on-demand absorbs read/write spikes with no capacity planning (trivial in core)
API Gateway request throttling caps protect downstream (trivial in core)
Lambda reserved concurrency bounds blast radius and guarantees headroom (trivial in core)
CloudFront caching offloads read-heavy GETs from the origin (trivial in core)
option: Lambda provisioned concurrency to remove cold starts for latency-sensitive spikes
option: SQS buffering in front of Lambda to smooth very large write bursts

name: Container API
whenToUse: Steady or high sustained traffic, long-running or CPU-bound work, or existing containerized services that need more control than Lambda's runtime model allows.
burstMechanisms: Fargate service auto-scaling on CPU/memory and ALB request-count targets (trivial in core)
ALB spreads load across tasks across AZs (trivial in core)
CloudFront caching offloads read traffic from the origin (trivial in core)
ElastiCache absorbs hot read bursts and protects the database (trivial in core)
option: keep a minimum warm task count / over-provision for sub-minute spikes auto-scaling cannot catch
option: provisioned RDS read replicas for read-heavy growth

name: Queue-Based Async Processing
whenToUse: Work that can be processed asynchronously (uploads, notifications, ETL, webhooks). Decouples producers from consumers so spikes are buffered instead of dropped, with built-in retries.
burstMechanisms: SQS buffers producer spikes and decouples ingest from processing rate (trivial in core)
DynamoDB on-demand absorbs write spikes from the worker (trivial in core)
Lambda reserved concurrency on the worker controls drain rate and protects downstream (trivial in core)
SNS fan-out distributes events to multiple consumers without coupling
option: SQS dead-letter queue + redrive for poison messages
option: provisioned worker concurrency to cap throughput against a rate-limited dependency

name: Static Site + API
whenToUse: A single-page app or static frontend served from the edge, with a small dynamic API behind it. Cheapest robust pattern for content-heavy sites with light backend needs.
burstMechanisms: CloudFront caching serves static assets from the edge and absorbs read bursts (trivial in core)
API Gateway throttling caps protect the dynamic backend (trivial in core)
DynamoDB on-demand absorbs API write spikes (trivial in core)
option: API Gateway response caching for hot read endpoints
option: Lambda provisioned concurrency if the API is latency-sensitive under spikes
